questions.question: A cargo ship travels $120$ miles in $4$ hours. What is its unit rate in miles per hour?
If a truck driver can deliver $5$ packages in $2$ hours, how many packages can be delivered in $10$ hours at the same rate?

A traffic flow model shows that the number of cars passing through an intersection varies directly with the time of day. If $100$ cars pass through in $2$ hours, how many cars will pass through in $5$ hours?
A shipping company charges $$0.50$ per pound to deliver packages. What is the total cost to deliver a $10$-pound package?
A bike messenger travels $12$ miles in $2$ hours. What is the messenger's unit rate in miles per hour?
If a plane flies $480$ miles in $4$ hours, how many miles will it fly in $7$ hours at the same rate?
A freight train can carry $20$ tons of cargo per car. If the train has $15$ cars, what is the total amount of cargo it can carry?
A bus travels $25$ miles in $1$ hour. What is its unit rate in miles per hour?

A delivery company has two types of trucks: one travels at $40$ mph and the other at $60$ mph. If the company needs to deliver $200$ packages, with each truck able to carry $20$ packages, and the distance to the delivery location is $120$ miles, how many hours will it take to deliver all packages using both trucks?

A traffic engineer is designing a new highway system. The engineer wants to ensure that the traffic flow rate is constant throughout the day. If the traffic flow model shows that $150$ cars pass through the highway in $3$ hours during peak hours, what is the unit rate of cars per hour? Additionally, if the highway has a capacity of $500$ cars per hour, what percentage of the highway's capacity is being utilized during peak hours?

teacher_rules: Consider using real-world examples to illustrate concepts
Emphasize the importance of unit rates in transportation and logistics
